title: alnus acutissima
intro: Alnus acutissima, also known as Japanese alder or sharp-leaved alder, is a species of deciduous tree native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. It has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Alnus acutissima can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its bark can also be used to make a traditional Japanese folk medicine called "kanjo" or "kankyo".
